rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE RICHARD WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/9/20. (No earlier date available.) The amendment to the second amended petition was filed on 1/15/20, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. The only proper form of notice is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. The revised proposed order (which should have been captioned, simply, "Order for Final Distribution," etc., not "Amended [Proposed] Order," etc., as no order has been entered yet to be amended and an unsigned order is, by its very nature, a proposed order) contains a slightly different version of the testamentary trust from that set forth in the second amended petition but not set forth in the amendment to the second amended petition. (The amendment to the second amended petition should have amended the testamentary trust terms and those very same terms should then have been reflected in the proposed order, verbatim.) The Court did not instruct the executor to include more or less language in the proposed order; the proposed order should accurately reflect what is in a verified pleading, not remove whole sections of a proposed trust. The attorney has not yet returned fees paid without a Court order. The personal representative, as pointed out numerous times, is the executor, not the administrator...

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEBORAH WHALLEY-KENDRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (4763 19th Street, San Francisco, Ca 94114-2316)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for sale hearings, even when an amended petition has defects. The amended petition was not filed at least 15 days before this hearing. No proper notice of intent to sell has been filed yet. (Notice of the sale confirmation hearing is not the notice of intent to sell.) No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing (using the mandatory Judicial Council form) has been filed. Notice still has not been given to the buyers. No reappraisal for sale has been filed, although it has been more than one year since the date of death. No courtesy copies of the amended petition or related papers have been provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALLIE CHRISTINE OWENSBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/3/20 to be heard with first filed petition in case PES-19-303279. Issues raised by the Examiner to whom this case was originally assigned have not yet been addressed. No courtesy copies or proposed order have been received. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.6 A., and 14.93 G.] No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed yet, clearly showing that the petitioner is seeking to have a lost will admitted. No proof of publication has been filed (which must also clearly indicate that the will is lost). No proof of holographic instrument has been filed. If issues are not addressed in time to deliver courtesy copies of all filed papers and the proposed order to the Examiner by 1/27/20, the hearing will be continued again.
